name: Baseline-005: TF-IDF + linear classifier
description: Classical NLP baseline: word 1-2-gram TF-IDF features over the question text, balanced one-vs-rest logistic regression on top. No model weights to download (unlike Baseline-002's sentence-transformer), no GPU. Trains on the same corpus as Baseline-002 (ICT receipts + curated truth controls; curated folklore held out as cross-corpus test). Tests whether classical bag-of-tokens can pick up the dark-core signature in question text alone.
notes: Genuinely worse than Baseline-002 (sentence-transformer + LR) on every folklore-axis metric — K1 0.30 vs 0.42, K3 0.22 vs 0.36, cross-corpus recall 0.067 vs 0.167. Suggests classical bag-of-tokens lacks the semantic abstraction needed to pick up folklore signatures, while embedding-based methods get at least a partial signal. K2 accuracy passes only because the truth class boundary is linguistically obvious (truth questions tend to have specific named entities; folklore questions don't follow a single lexical pattern).
